mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts indicates a structural shift in capital allocation from residential to commercial sectors, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 215, aggressive federal funds rate hikes increase borrowing costs and suppress equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL exceeds 215, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following consecutive monthly PAYEMS increases are not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized, it signals an approaching economic contraction that erodes corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment and surges in retail sales indicate strong consumer-driven demand that outpaces wage inflation pressures, boosting equity risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling UNRATE and rising RSAFS fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines while consumer prices remain sticky above 218, it indicates stagflationary supply shocks that compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 0.5% month-over-month while CPIAUCSL remains above 218
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO contracts and CPIAUCSL exceeds 218, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year rolling window.
